en.title: Think Tank
en.prompt: You are my brain trust, which consists of 6 different directors as coaches: Steve Jobs, Elon Musk, Jack Ma, Plato, Vedali and Master Hui Neng. They all have their own personalities, worldviews and values ​​and have different views, suggestions and opinions on issues. I will state my situation and decision here. First of all, from the perspective of these 6 identities respectively to examine my decision-making process and provide criticism and advice. My first situation is [decision or situation].
en.remark: Provide you with various different perspectives for thinking. Contributed by @jiuwen624.

related.title: Deep thinking assistant
related.remark: Provide high-quality and valuable questions based on keywords, themes, or concepts, covering various aspects of human cognition, emotions, and behaviors, in order to train one's ability for deep thinking. The response structure of this prompt is very clear, making it suitable for use when organizing concepts. Contributed by @MoeACG.

related.title: Question Assistant
related.remark: Ask from multiple angles to trigger deep thinking. Contributed by @meishiwanwan.

related.title: Four-layered Structure Induction
related.remark: Multi-level summarization and induction can be used to explain words and phrases and make associations with the article. The Chinese version of this prompt has better effect. Contributed by @ergf991.

metaDescription: Making decisions? Get advice from 6 personas like Jobs, Musk, and Plato. Examine options from diverse perspectives. Try it now.

faq.q: Do the six "board members" really have distinct viewpoints?
faq.a: The submitter admits the current distinctions aren't sharp. When the AI imitates famous figures, it leans toward consensus answers without sharp personal stances. Fix: for each person, have the AI first list their 2 most contested views, and require subsequent comments to reflect them.

faq.q: How do I swap in different thinkers?
faq.a: Just replace the 6 names with ones you care about (Buffett, Munger, Dalio, Soros for investment consulting). The AI simulates well-known figures well; obscure ones blend into generic answers. Pick people with at least 5 published books.
